documents.bodyTerms: this development tutorial for ebpf based (compile once, run everywhere). provides practical practices from beginner advanced, including basic concepts, code examples, and real world applications. unlike bcc, use frameworks like libbpf, cilium, libbpf rs, eunomia bpf development, with examples languages such go, rust. does not cover complex concepts scenario introductions. its main purpose provide tools very short, starting twenty lines code! help application developers quickly grasp methods techniques. the content can found directory, each directory being independent tool example. complete source tutorial, please refer repo https://github.com/eunomia bpf/bpf developer github. you find helpful, give star! table contents getting started section contains simple program primarily utilizes framework simplify introduces usage process ebpf. lesson 0 introduce introduction core 1 helloworld hello world, 2 kprobe unlink monitoring system calls 3 fentry 4 opensnoop capturing opening files filter global variables 5 uprobe bashreadline readline function 6 sigsnoop signal sending store state hash maps 7 execsnoop execution, output perf event array 8 exitsnoop exit events, ring buffer 9 runqlat scheduling latency recording histogram 10 hardirqs interrupts softirqs advanced documents start build projects mainly combine them various scenarios use. 11 bootstrap develop user space programs trace exec() exit() 12

documents.bodyTerms: you may find more about ebpf these places: curated list awesome projects related ebpf: website and tutorials: the complete source code: this also papers read recent years, might helpful for people who are interested research. (extended berkeley packet filter) emerging technology that allows safe execution user provided programs linux kernel. has gained widespread adoption years accelerating network processing, enhancing observability, enabling programmable processing. document some key research over past few years. cover several aspects ebpf, including distributed systems, storage, networking, formally verifying jit compiler verifier, applying intrusion detection, automatically generating hardware designs from programs. highlights: enables executing custom functions kernel accelerate protocols, storage engines, networking applications with improved throughput lower latency compared traditional userspace implementations. formal verification components like verifier ensures correctness reveals bugs real world ebpf's programmability efficiency make suitable building detection monitoring entirely automated synthesis software developers quickly generate optimized processing pipelines cards. demonstrate versatility security, simplifying programming. grows, important area systems many open problems performance, safety, integration, ease use. have any suggestions adding papers, please feel free issue pr. was created 2023.10, new will added future. check out our eunomia bpf tutorials developer tutorial. i'm looking phd position 2024/2025.

documents.title: eBPF Tutorial by Example 19: Security Detection and Defense using LSM
documents.description: eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) is a powerful network and performance analysis tool widely used in the Linux kernel. eBPF allows developers to dynamically load, update, and run user-defined code without restarting
documents.titleText: ebpf tutorial by example 19: security detection and defense using lsm
documents.descriptionText: ebpf (extended berkeley packet filter) is a powerful network and performance analysis tool widely used in the linux kernel. ebpf allows developers to dynamically load, update, and run user-defined code without restarting ebpf (extended berkeley packet filter) is a powerful network and performance analysis tool widely used in the linux kernel. ebpf allows developers to dynamically load, update, and run user-defined code without restarting
documents.bodyTerms: ebpf (extended berkeley packet filter) powerful network and performance analysis tool widely used the linux kernel. allows developers dynamically load, update, run user defined code without restarting kernel modifying source code. this feature enables provide high flexibility performance, making applicable system analysis. same applies applications security, article will introduce how use lsm (linux security modules) mechanism implement simple check program. complete code: background has been official framework since 2.6, implementations based include selinux apparmor. with introduction bpf 5.7, have able freely function level checks. provides example limiting access specific ipv4 address through socket connect using (this demonstrates its control precision.) overview that supports various computer models. predefines set hook points critical paths related decoupling from modules. different modules loaded/unloaded existing code, thus enabling them inspection features. past, mainly involved configuring like apparmor writing custom however, everything changed. now, can write policies load into mount some currently supported include: file open, creation, deletion, movement; filesystem mounting; operations tasks processes; sockets (creating, binding sockets, sending receiving messages, etc.); for more points, refer hooks.h. verifying availability first, please confirm your version higher than 5.7. next, you following command support enabled: cat /boot/config $(uname grep config

documents.description: Linux's Traffic Control (tc) subsystem has been present in the kernel for many years. Similar to the relationship between iptables and netfilter, tc includes a user-space tc program and a kernel-level traffic control fra
documents.titleText: ebpf tutorial by example 20: tc traffic control
documents.descriptionText: linux's traffic control (tc) subsystem has been present in the kernel for many years. similar to the relationship between iptables and netfilter, tc includes a user-space tc program and a kernel-level traffic control fra linux's traffic control (tc) subsystem has been present in the kernel for many years. similar to the relationship between iptables and netfilter, tc includes a user-space tc program and a kernel-level traffic control fra
documents.bodyTerms: linux's traffic control (tc) subsystem has been present the kernel for many years. similar relationship between iptables and netfilter, includes user space program level framework. mainly used sending receiving packets terms rate, sequence, other aspects. starting from linux 4.1, added some new attachment points supports loading ebpf programs filters onto these points. complete source code: overview protocol stack perspective, located link layer. its position already completed allocation buff later than xdp. order packets, uses queue structure temporarily store organize packets. subsystem, corresponding data algorithm mechanism are abstracted qdisc (queueing discipline). exposes two callback interfaces enqueuing dequeuing externally, internally hides implementation queuing algorithms. qdisc, can implement complex tree structures based classes. mounted class specific filtering logic, return value determines whether packet belongs class. when reaches top enqueue interface called, executed one until filter matches successfully. then sent pointed that enters processing process configured framework provides called classifier action mechanism, is, filter, packet, implementing classification mechanism. existing with direct mode, which allows loaded values such act actions, instead just returning classid like traditional handing over module. now, qdiscs perform actions. writing include define 0 eth 0x0800 internet /// @tchook {"ifindex":1, "attach point":"bpf ingress"} @tcopts

documents.bodyTerms: this tutorial, will introduce xdp (express data path) and walk through small example help you get started. later on, explore more advanced applications, such load balancers, firewalls, other real world use cases. please give start github are interested ebpf xdp! the complete source code: what xdp? high performance, programmable path linux kernel, designed for packet processing network interface level. attaching programs directly device drivers, can intercept handle packets before they reach kernel’s networking stack. allows extremely low latency efficient processing, making ideal tasks like ddos defense, balancing, traffic filtering. fact, achieve throughput 24 million per second (mpps) core why operates lower level than traditional components, cbpf (classic bpf), running inside soft interrupt context driver. even processed kernel's standard stack, bypassing creation skb buff structure, which represents linux. early stage provides significant performance gains simple but frequent operations dropping malicious balancing across servers. compared mechanisms, strikes balance between usability, leveraging security reliability kernel while providing flexibility ebpf. overview vs. approaches xdp, several solutions aimed accelerate entirely. one prominent dpdk (data plane development kit). user space applications take direct control devices, achieving very performance. however, approach comes with trade offs: 1. lack integration bypass

documents.descriptionText: ever plugged in a new mouse or drawing tablet only to find it doesn't work quite right on linux? maybe the y-axis is inverted, buttons are mapped wrong, or the device just feels broken. traditionally, fixing these quirks ever plugged in a new mouse or drawing tablet only to find it doesn't work quite right on linux? maybe the y-axis is inverted, buttons are mapped wrong, or the device just feels broken. traditionally, fixing these quirks
documents.bodyTerms: ever plugged new mouse drawing tablet only find doesn't work quite right linux? maybe the axis inverted, buttons are mapped wrong, device just feels broken. traditionally, fixing these quirks meant writing kernel driver, waiting weeks for review, and hoping your distro ships fix sometime next year. then, you've probably bought different device. this tutorial shows you better way. we'll use hid bpf create virtual modify its input fly using ebpf. minutes, not months, you'll see how without touching kernel. same technology now shipping with 14+ fixes mainline linux complete source code: problem (human interface device) standard protocol devices like mice, keyboards, game controllers, tablets. well defined, but hardware vendors often implement incorrectly add that don't follow spec. when happens linux, users suffer. let's say buy inverted axis. move stylus up, cursor goes down. get where 4 5 report themselves 6 7, breaking browser's back/forward navigation. bugs incredibly frustrating because works perfectly other operating systems, sees raw incorrect data. traditional requires driver patching existing one. you'd need understand development, submit patches lkml, them reviewed, wait release, then again distribution ship user broken hardware, could take six months more. most return dual boot another

documents.description: Ever tried attaching multiple BPF programs to the TC ingress path and got frustrated managing qdisc handles, filter priorities, and the tc CLI? Or needed one application's TC program to coexist safely with another's with
documents.titleText: ebpf tutorial by example 50: composable traffic control with tcx links
documents.descriptionText: ever tried attaching multiple bpf programs to the tc ingress path and got frustrated managing qdisc handles, filter priorities, and the tc cli? or needed one application's tc program to coexist safely with another's with ever tried attaching multiple bpf programs to the tc ingress path and got frustrated managing qdisc handles, filter priorities, and the tc cli? or needed one application's tc program to coexist safely with another's with
documents.bodyTerms: ever tried attaching multiple bpf programs the ingress path and got frustrated managing qdisc handles, filter priorities, cli? needed one application's program coexist safely with another's without accidentally overwriting it? traditional cls attachment through works, but inherits decades queueing discipline plumbing that was never designed for centric world. what you could attach, order, manage using same link based api xdp cgroup already enjoy? this tcx (traffic control extension) solves. introduced daniel borkmann merged linux 6.6, provides lightweight, multi attach infrastructure egress data path. get semantics (safe ownership, auto detachment close, explicit ordering before after flags) touching single priority. tutorial, we'll two loopback interface, place other, query kernel's live chain state, generate traffic verify execution order. complete source code: introduction tcx: why classic rethink problem: unsafe ownership (cls bpf) bolted onto existing framework. program, first clsact then added handle worked fine operator, created real problems cloud native environments where applications need interface: 1. model del from application can remove another program. there's protection against because filters are identified handle/priority, not process them. 2. priority conflicts might pick number. second silently replaces first. 3. permanent default persist until explicitly removed. attached crashes cleanup, remains,

documents.titleText: the bpftrace one-liner tutorial
documents.descriptionText: this teaches you bpftrace for linux in 12 easy lessons, where each lesson is a one-liner you can try running. this series of one-liners introduces concepts which are summarized as bullet points. for a full reference to b this teaches you bpftrace for linux in 12 easy lessons, where each lesson is a one-liner you can try running. this series of one-liners introduces concepts which are summarized as bullet points. for a full reference to b
documents.bodyTerms: this teaches you bpftrace for linux 12 easy lessons, where each lesson one liner can try running. series liners introduces concepts which are summarized bullet points. full reference bpftrace, see the man page contributed brendan gregg, netflix (2018), based his freebsd dtrace tutorial. 1. listing probes 'tracepointsys enter "bpftrace lists all probes, and search term added. probe instrumentation point capturing event data. supplied supports wildcards/globs also piped grep(1) regular expression searching. 2. hello world 'begin printf("hello world\n"); attaching 1 probe... prints welcome message. run it, then hit ctrl end. word begin special that fires start program (like awk's begin). use set variables print headers. action associated with example calls printf() when fires. 3. file opens openat printf("%s %s\n", comm, str(args.filename)); snmp pass /proc/cpuinfo /proc/stat snmpd /proc/net/dev /proc/net/if inet6 traces they happen, we're printing process name pathname. begins tracepointsys openat: tracepoint type (kernel static tracing), instrumenting openat() syscall (is entered). tracepoints preferred over kprobes dynamic tracing, introduced 6), since have stable api. note: modern systems (glibc 2.26) open wrapper always syscall. comm builtin variable has current process's name. other similar builtins include pid tid. args struct containing arguments. automatically generated information. members found

documents.title: eBPF Tutorial: cgroup-based Policy Control
documents.description: Do you need to enforce network access control on containers or specific process groups without affecting the entire system? Or do you need to restrict certain processes from accessing specific devices while allowing othe
documents.titleText: ebpf tutorial: cgroup-based policy control
documents.descriptionText: do you need to enforce network access control on containers or specific process groups without affecting the entire system? or do you need to restrict certain processes from accessing specific devices while allowing othe do you need to enforce network access control on containers or specific process groups without affecting the entire system? or do you need to restrict certain processes from accessing specific devices while allowing othe
documents.bodyTerms: you need enforce network access control containers specific process groups without affecting the entire system? restrict certain processes from accessing devices while allowing others use them normally? traditional iptables and device permissions are global, making fine grained per group impossible. this problem cgroup ebpf solves. attaching programs cgroups (control groups), can implement policy based membership—only belonging affected. enables container isolation, multi tenant security, sandbox environments. tutorial, we'll build complete "policy guard" program that demonstrates tcp connection filtering, control, sysctl read restrictions—three types usage. what ebpf? core idea simple: attach cgroup, all will controlled program. unlike xdp/tc which filter traffic interface, filters membership—put program, container's access, reads/writes under your control. other completely unaffected. model perfect for scenarios. kubernetes networkpolicy uses hood. also isolation (e.g., restricting gpus), security sandboxes (preventing reads sensitive sysctls), more. when denies operation, userspace syscalls return eperm (operation not permitted). hook points 1. bpf prog type sock addr socket address hooks triggered (bind/connect/sendmsg/recvmsg): section name description ipv4 bind cgroup/bind4 bind() calls ipv6 cgroup/bind6 connect cgroup/connect4 connect() cgroup/connect6 udp sendmsg cgroup/sendmsg4, cgroup/sendmsg6 sends recvmsg cgroup/recvmsg4, cgroup/recvmsg6 receives unix cgroup/connect context struct contains user ip4, port (network byte order) semantics 1 allow,

documents.descriptionText: ever tried building a linked list in ebpf and got stuck using awkward integer indices instead of real pointers? or needed to share large amounts of data between your kernel bpf program and userspace without expensive sys ever tried building a linked list in ebpf and got stuck using awkward integer indices instead of real pointers? or needed to share large amounts of data between your kernel bpf program and userspace without expensive sys
documents.bodyTerms: ever tried building linked list ebpf and got stuck using awkward integer indices instead real pointers? needed share large amounts data between your kernel bpf program userspace without expensive syscalls? traditional maps force you work around pointer limitations require system calls for every access. what could just use normal pointers have direct memory access from both userspace? this arena solves. created alexei starovoitov 2024, provides sparse shared region where programs can build complex structures like lists, trees, graphs, while gets zero copy the same memory. tutorial, we'll show how manipulate standard operations. complete source code: introduction arena: breaking free map problem: when aren't enough are fantastic simple key value storage, but they fundamental need scale sharing. let's look developers faced before existed. ring buffers only one direction send userspace, can't write back. they're streaming only, random hash array syscalls lookup elem() userspace. allocate all their upfront, wasting space fraction entries. most critically, you're forced link together. old way looked mess: struct node int next idx; pointers, must index! data; uint(type, type array); uint(max entries, 10000); type(value, node); nodes sec(".maps"); traverse requires repeated lookups idx head (idx 1) elem(&nodes map, &idx); (!n) break;

documents.bodyTerms: ever written ebpf packet parser and struggled with those verbose data end bounds checks that the verifier still rejects? tried send variable length events through ring buffers only find yourself locked into fixed size structures? traditional development forces you prove memory safety statically compile time, which becomes painful when dealing runtime determined sizes like lengths user configurable snapshot lengths. this what bpf dynptrs (dynamic pointers) solve. introduced gradually from linux v5.19, provide friendly way work shifting some checking time static analysis validation. tutorial, we'll build ingress program uses skb parse tcp packets safely ringbuf output containing payload snapshots. complete source code: introduction dynamic pointers problem: verification isn't enough verifier's core mission proving load time. every pointer dereference must bounded, array access within limits. works beautifully for simple cases, but struggle are runtime. consider parsing where header comes 4 bit field, reading amounts payload. classic approach requires extensive comparisons, even correctly code sometimes fails because cannot trace all possible paths. working non linear (paged buffers), situation gets worse since directly accessible ctx all. presents similar challenges. reserve() returns raw pointer, writing makes uncomfortable your writes stay bounds. solution: checked introduce opaque handle type

documents.bodyTerms: have you ever wanted extend kernel behavior—like adding custom scheduler, network protocol, security policy—but were put off the complexity writing and maintaining full module? what could define logic directly ebpf, with dynamic updates, safe execution, programmable control, all without recompiling risking system stability? this power bpf struct ops advanced ebpf feature allows programs implement callbacks operations structure, effectively letting "plug in" subsystems. goes beyond simple tracing filtering—you can now core bpf. for example, use gpu scheduling memory offloading extensions drivers (see lpc 2024 talk ext project). tutorial, will explore how dynamically subsystem behavior. won't using common tcp congestion control example. instead, we'll take more fundamental approach that mirrors extensibility seen kfuncs. create module defines new, set operations. act placeholder, creating new attachment points our programs. then, write program these demonstrates powerful pattern: minimal expose interface, then provide full, complex implementation. complete source code tutorial found here: introduction ops: subsystems challenge: extending behavior safely traditionally, functionality linux kernel, such file system, scheduler algorithm, requires module. while powerful, modules come significant challenges: complexity: development has steep learning curve deep understanding internals. safety: bug easily crash entire system. there are sandboxing guarantees. maintenance: must maintained

documents.descriptionText: this is a development tutorial for ebpf based on co-re (compile once, run everywhere). it provides practical ebpf development practices from beginner to advanced, including basic concepts, code examples, and real-world a this is a development tutorial for ebpf based on co-re (compile once, run everywhere). it provides practical ebpf development practices from beginner to advanced, including basic concepts, code examples, and real-world a
documents.bodyTerms: this development tutorial for ebpf based (compile once, run everywhere). provides practical practices from beginner advanced, including basic concepts, code examples, and real world applications. unlike bcc, use frameworks like libbpf, cilium, libbpf rs, eunomia bpf development, with examples languages such go, rust. does not cover complex concepts scenario introductions. its main purpose provide tools very short, starting twenty lines code! help application developers quickly grasp methods techniques. the content can found directory, each directory being independent tool example. complete source tutorial, please refer repo https://github.com/eunomia bpf/bpf developer github. you find helpful, give star! table contents getting started section contains simple program primarily utilizes framework simplify introduces usage process ebpf. lesson 0 introduce introduction core 1 helloworld hello world, 2 kprobe unlink monitoring system calls 3 fentry 4 opensnoop capturing opening files filter global variables 5 uprobe bashreadline readline function 6 sigsnoop signal sending store state hash maps 7 execsnoop execution, output perf event array 8 exitsnoop exit events, ring buffer 9 runqlat scheduling latency recording histogram 10 hardirqs interrupts softirqs advanced documents start build projects mainly combine them various scenarios use. 11 bootstrap develop user space programs trace exec() exit() 12

documents.title: eBPF by Example: Building a GPU Flamegraph Profiler with CUPTI
documents.description: Have you ever wondered which part of your CPU code is responsible for launching a specific GPU kernel? CPU profilers can show you the host-side call stacks, but they lose visibility once the work is handed off to the GPU
documents.titleText: ebpf by example: building a gpu flamegraph profiler with cupti
documents.descriptionText: have you ever wondered which part of your cpu code is responsible for launching a specific gpu kernel? cpu profilers can show you the host-side call stacks, but they lose visibility once the work is handed off to the gpu have you ever wondered which part of your cpu code is responsible for launching a specific gpu kernel? cpu profilers can show you the host-side call stacks, but they lose visibility once the work is handed off to the gpu
documents.bodyTerms: have you ever wondered which part your cpu code responsible for launching specific gpu kernel? profilers can show the host side call stacks, but they lose visibility once work handed off gpu. other hand, detail what's happening device often don't link back function that initiated it. this creates blind spot, making difficult answer critical question: "which line causing slow kernel run?" tutorial will guide through building profiler bridges gap. create unified flamegraph using power ebpf and nvidia's cupti (cuda profiling tools interface). end, you'll tool captures stack traces moment cudalaunchkernel() intelligently stitches them together with corresponding kernel's execution data. result powerful visualization reveals exactly paths are triggering kernels, allowing pinpoint performance bottlenecks without recompiling application. we'll achieve cupti's correlation ids, act bridge connecting api calls their executions. real world example: qwen3 llm inference see our action, let's look 0.6b large language model during inference. resulting flamegraph, shown below, visualizes entire operation, merging stacks kernels launch. immediately becomes clear matmul (matrix multiplication) most time consuming part, accounting 95% total time. !qwen3 key insights from flamegraph: gives breakdown where spending its time: 3.1 seconds (95% time). tells matrix multiplication is, far, biggest bottleneck. multi

documents.title: eBPF Tutorial by Example: Tracing Intel NPU Kernel Driver Operations
documents.description: Neural Processing Units (NPUs) are the next frontier in AI acceleration - built directly into modern CPUs to handle machine learning workloads without burning through GPU power budgets. Intel's Lunar Lake and Meteor Lake
documents.titleText: ebpf tutorial by example: tracing intel npu kernel driver operations
documents.descriptionText: neural processing units (npus) are the next frontier in ai acceleration - built directly into modern cpus to handle machine learning workloads without burning through gpu power budgets. intel's lunar lake and meteor lake neural processing units (npus) are the next frontier in ai acceleration - built directly into modern cpus to handle machine learning workloads without burning through gpu power budgets. intel's lunar lake and meteor lake
documents.bodyTerms: neural processing units (npus) are the next frontier acceleration built directly into modern cpus handle machine learning workloads without burning through gpu power budgets. intel's lunar lake and meteor processors pack dedicated npu hardware, but when your model runs slow, inference fails, memory allocation crashes, debugging feels impossible. driver black box, firmware communication opaque, userspace apis hide what's really happening kernel. this tutorial shows you how trace intel kernel operations using ebpf bpftrace. we'll monitor complete workflow from level zero api calls down functions, track ipc with firmware, measure patterns, diagnose performance bottlenecks. end, you'll understand drivers work internally have practical tools for workload issues. source code: architecture follows two layer similar drivers. module (intel vpu) lives mainline linux drivers/accel/ivpu/ exposes /dev/accel/accel0 device interface. handles hardware communication, management mmu, (inter processor communication) running accelerator itself. (libze vpu.so) implements api, unified programming interface accelerators. call functions like zememallochost() zecommandqueueexecutecommandlists(), library translates these drm ioctls that hit module. validates requests, sets mappings, submits polls completion. itself autonomously hardware. receives command buffers kernel, schedules compute kernels, manages chip memory, signals completion interrupts. all happens via channels, which shared regions where exchange messages. means three layers

documents.title: AgentCgroup: What Happens When AI Coding Agents Meet OS Resources?
documents.description: AgentCgroup: What Happens When AI Coding Agents Meet OS Resources?
documents.titleText: agentcgroup: what happens when ai coding agents meet os resources?
documents.descriptionText: agentcgroup: what happens when ai coding agents meet os resources? agentcgroup: what happens when ai coding agents meet os resources?
documents.bodyTerms: coding agents such claude code, openhands, and swe agent are increasingly deployed multi tenant cloud environments, where they execute diverse tool calls inside sandboxed containers. despite growing adoption, the level resource dynamics these workloads remain poorly understood. present first systematic characterization, analyzing 144 software engineering tasks from rebench benchmark across two llm backends. our measurements reveal that overhead, including container initialization execution, accounts for 56–74% end latency, while reasoning contributes only 26–44%. memory exhibits 15.4x peak average ratio (compared 1.5x serverless 2–3x microservices), with change rates reaching 3 gb/s sub second bursts. same type (bash) varies 13.7x consumption depending command semantics, repeated runs task produce 1.8x execution time variance near zero correlation −0.14) between token output memory. characteristics expose mismatches existing management mechanisms, kernel cgroup limits systemd oomd kubernetes vpa, static allocation either wastes 93% provisioned capacity triggers oom kills destroy minutes accumulated, non reproducible state. this post, summarize characterization findings agentcgroup paper describe how ebpf based enforcement can bridge gap workload control. paper: agentcgroup: understanding controlling resources github: https://github.com/yunwei37/agentcgroup what did instrumented production agent, running backends: haiku 4.5 (cloud api): inference anthropic's cloud; framework calls. glm 4.7 flash (local gpu): local

documents.title: Reverse Engineering Claude Code's SSL Traffic with eBPF
documents.description: Reverse Engineering Claude Code's SSL Traffic with eBPF
documents.titleText: reverse engineering claude code's ssl traffic with ebpf
documents.descriptionText: reverse engineering claude code's ssl traffic with ebpf reverse engineering claude code's ssl traffic with ebpf
documents.bodyTerms: ever wondered what your coding agent actually sending over the network? part our work agentsight ebpf powered observability tool that monitors agents system boundaries without modifying their code needed intercept claude code's encrypted api traffic understand its communication patterns. can capture both intends (by intercepting llm traffic) and does monitoring kernel events), then correlate two into complete causal chain. make this work, first need crack open agent's network traffic. sounds straightforward, right? just hook node.js's openssl with sslsniff, like did older versions code. back then, ran node.js dynamically linked libssl.so, standard based ssl hooking worked out box. but everything changed. new 213 stripped bun binary boringssl statically all symbols removed. write symbol tools are completely blind it. any self respecting systems researcher would do: asked reverse engineer itself. yes, very were trying monitor became engineering assistant, analyzing own binary, searching for byte patterns, helping locate functions inside post chronicles journey: from discovering why fail, matching patterns against bun's source profile builds, finally capturing every single call including full /v1/messages conversation stream prompts, responses, sse events. along way, chased fascinating red herring about "dual tls paths" turned simple timing issue. techniques here apply

documents.title: A Taxonomy of GPU Bugs: 19 Defect Classes for CUDA Verification
documents.description: A Taxonomy of GPU Bugs: 19 Defect Classes for CUDA Verification
documents.titleText: a taxonomy of gpu bugs: 19 defect classes for cuda verification
documents.descriptionText: a taxonomy of gpu bugs: 19 defect classes for cuda verification a taxonomy of gpu bugs: 19 defect classes for cuda verification
documents.bodyTerms: introduction gpu programming introduces distinct class correctness and performance challenges that differ fundamentally from traditional cpu based systems. the simt (single instruction, multiple threads) execution model, hierarchical memory architecture, massive parallelism create unique bug patterns require specialized verification detection techniques. just ebpf enables safe, verified extension code run inside linux kernel, bpftime ext (the arxiv, previous name egpu) bring gpus, allowing user defined policy (for observability, scheduling, resource control) injected into drivers kernels with static guarantees such framework must ensure cannot introduce crashes, hangs, data races, unbounded overhead. critical concern modern deployments interference multi tenant environments contention for shared resources makes time unpredictable. "making powerful enemies nvidia gpus" studies how adversarial can amplify slowdowns, arguing system level safety property when gpus are shared. this motivates treating bounded overhead property, not merely optimization goal. build sound verifier, first understand what wrong. taxonomy identifies defect classes verifier address, drawing lessons ebpf's success: restrict enforce execution, verify before loading. synthesize findings verifiers (gpuverify, gklee, esbmc gpu), dynamic detectors (compute sanitizer, simulee, cusan), empirical (wu al., scord, iguard) 19 organized along two dimensions: impact type (safety, correctness, performance) specificity (gpu specific, amplified, shared). each entry provides

documents.title: Architectures for Agent Systems: A Survey of Isolation, Integration, and Governance
documents.description: Architectures for Agent Systems: A Survey of Isolation, Integration, and Governance
documents.titleText: architectures for agent systems: a survey of isolation, integration, and governance
documents.descriptionText: architectures for agent systems: a survey of isolation, integration, and governance architectures for agent systems: a survey of isolation, integration, and governance
documents.bodyTerms: large language model (llm) based agent systems software that leverages llms autonomously plan and execute multi step tasks using external tools are rapidly moving from proof concept demos into enterprise deployment. these agents promise automate coding, operations, data analysis, more, but deploying them production raises new challenges security, reliability, integration. over the last half year, community has converged key strategies: strong isolation for executing untrusted actions, standardized protocols tool integration, governance frameworks align behavior with policies. this survey provides systematic review recent developments (roughly latter 2025), including sandbox architectures, emerging standards like mcp, open source projects, industry initiatives, research advances. focus pain points encountered when bringing how latest solutions address (or still fall short on) those needs. 1. system architecture ready typically consists several layers: (i) llm reasoning core (the "agent" decides which actions take), (ii) interface invoke services (e.g. via apis, command line, databases), (iii) execution environment runtime where agent's (like running code shell commands) actually occur. surrounding components memory/state storage, orchestration (especially multiple work together), monitoring control (for safety compliance). overarching architectural challenge highly dynamic ended: may generate arbitrary requests runtime, often unpredictable input. requires different approach than traditional deterministic

documents.title: NVIDIA Open GPU Kernel Modules Comprehensive Source Code Analysis
documents.description: NVIDIA Open GPU Kernel Modules Comprehensive Source Code Analysis
documents.titleText: nvidia open gpu kernel modules comprehensive source code analysis
documents.descriptionText: nvidia open gpu kernel modules comprehensive source code analysis nvidia open gpu kernel modules comprehensive source code analysis
documents.bodyTerms: may 2022, nvidia made decision that would fundamentally alter the landscape gpu computing linux: they open sourced kernel mode components their driver. this wasn't simple code dump. instead, was release over 935,000 lines production quality, battle tested powers everything from consumer gaming rigs world's fastest supercomputers. for first time, developers, researchers, and engineers could peer inside machinery manages some most complex hardware ever created. document represents comprehensive deep dive into codebase, providing technical autopsy one sophisticated device drivers linux ecosystem. course analysis, we've examined every major subsystem, traced data flows through multiple abstraction layers, documented architectural decisions span decade evolution. what emerges not just driver, but masterclass systems programming: how manage heterogeneous resources, maintain binary compatibility across wildly different generations, balance competing demands performance, security, maintainability. version: 580.95.05 analysis date: 2025 10 13 license: dual mit/gpl repository: https://github.com/nvidia/open modules you'll discover reveals inner workings driver must simultaneously: support nine distinct architectures 2014's maxwell 2024's blackwell, each with capabilities provide unified memory semantics cpu address spaces, transparently migrating microsecond timescales drive displays 8k resolution hdr color while maintaining frame perfect timing coordinate high speed interconnects capable 150 gb/s per link between dozens gpus

documents.description: AgentSight: Keeping Your AI Agents Under Control with eBPF-Powered System Observability
documents.titleText: agentsight: keeping your ai agents under control with ebpf-powered system observability
documents.descriptionText: agentsight: keeping your ai agents under control with ebpf-powered system observability agentsight: keeping your ai agents under control with ebpf-powered system observability
documents.bodyTerms: picture this: your agent autonomously writing code and executing commands, but you have idea what it's actually doing. sounds bit unsettling, right? llm agents like claude gemini cli become increasingly prevalent, we're handing more control ai. here's the catch: these are fundamentally different from traditional software, our existing monitoring tools struggling keep up. they can either see "thinking" (through prompts) "doing" (system calls), never both together. watching someone's mouth move seeing their actions, connecting two. this blind spot makes nearly impossible tell working normally, under attack, burning through budget infinite loop. that's why built agentsight. leverage ebpf monitor system boundaries where can't hide. agentsight intercepts encrypted communications understand intends do, monitors kernel events track does, then uses smart correlation engine connect dots into complete causal chain. best part? don't need modify code, works with any framework, performance overhead less than 3%! tests, successfully caught prompt injection attacks, spotted expensive reasoning loops before drained budgets, revealed hidden bottlenecks multi collaborations. now open source ready use arxiv paper available introduction witnessing revolution: longer just helper tool, becoming true participant. think about when cursor agent, cli, you're essentially letting directly system, creating processes, modifying files,

documents.description: Profiling and Tracing Tools Across System Layers and Architectures
documents.titleText: profiling and tracing tools across system layers and architectures
documents.descriptionText: profiling and tracing tools across system layers and architectures profiling and tracing tools across system layers and architectures
documents.bodyTerms: profiling and tracing are complementary techniques for analyzing software performance behavior. typically measures where program spends its time resources, aggregating data (e.g. cpu per function, memory usage module) identify bottlenecks hot spots. contrast, records timeline events operations function calls, kernel events, network requests) reconstruct execution flows. both motivated the need understand optimize complex systems: using these tools “vital understanding behavior, optimisation potentials” common use cases include finding hotspots code, diagnosing leaks i/o delays, concurrency issues lock contention), tracking requests across distributed services. applying tracing, developers researchers can observe internal state software, leading faster troubleshooting more efficient, reliable systems. modern environments, profiling/tracing spans multiple layers from low level application, runtime, system behaviors. sections below present comprehensive catalog frameworks each (covering open source commercial solutions), organized context. compare their capabilities major architectures (x86, arm, risc gpus, tpus, dsps, etc.), discuss design challenges (overhead, observability, correlation, etc.) that shape current research. typical scenarios highlighted illustrate why indispensable example, improving end user experience optimizing slow code paths, catching sporadic via continuous monitoring, microservice interactions pinpoint latency transaction. goal give broad overview deep insights into art technology. operating level, capture drivers scheduler context switches, irqs) diagnose performance.

documents.descriptionText: the modern memory testing arsenal -- a complete guide to benchmarking tools for next-gen memory systems the modern memory testing arsenal -- a complete guide to benchmarking tools for next-gen memory systems
documents.bodyTerms: introduction memory systems are evolving rapidly. from traditional ddr dram high bandwidth (hbm), persistent (pmem), and the emerging compute express link (cxl) technology, today's feature complex heterogeneous hierarchies that demand sophisticated evaluation approaches. this comprehensive guide surveys cutting edge tools methodologies available for testing, benchmarking, profiling modern systems. whether you're hardware architect designing next generation controllers, software developer optimizing applications memory, researcher exploring system design, survey provides roadmap essential shaping 2018 2025. cover everything synthetic workload generators can clone application behavior, trace replay frameworks enable reproducible specialized benchmark suites technologies, provide deep insights into performance bottlenecks. landscape has evolved simple latency measurements driven synthesis unified span multiple technologies. 1. proxy benchmarks create artificial access patterns statistically mimic real applications. they faster simulation protect proprietary code cloning behavior. key projects area include classic reuse distance based models recent approaches: west (hpca 2012) emulation using stochastic traces seminal black box technique replicate program’s data cache behavior researchgate.netresearchgate.net. profiles generates (“clone”) yields nearly identical miss statistics original workload. achieved <0.5% error ratio across 1000+ configurationsresearchgate.net. limitation: targeted caches only (temporal locality), requiring separate per configuration. stm 2014) spatial temporal extended modeling both locality (access strides)

documents.bodyTerms: the association for computing machinery's architectural support programming languages and operating systems (asplos) conference premier venue where researchers present cutting edge work spanning computer architecture, languages, systems, their intersections. asplos 2025 showcased significant advances across these domains, with particular emphasis heterogeneous computing, novel memory architectures. this summary analyzes key papers trends from conference, highlighting implications both academia industry. hot research directions emerging topics: many converge making massive scale practical efficient llm serving training moe experts, new privacy preserving (homomorphic encryption gpus, zero knowledge proofs acceleration). another clear thrust disaggregation cxl multiple works assume era composable (cxl enabled) propose os, file, transaction system designs exploit it. security remains vibrant but focus shifting classic os/network hardware centric microarchitectural issues like rowhammer, speculative execution, side channel resistant scheduling. serverless cloud native optimizations are also prominent: there’s interest bringing high performance techniques (gpu sharing, low latency networking, global scheduling) into containerized environments, indicating industry push toward combining flexibility abstractions. unsolved problems gaps: despite progress, underscore enduring challenges. wall data movement loom large whether inter gpu communication persistent overheads. several (e.g., anykey ssd, fusion object store) illustrate that storage still bottleneck custom solutions needed bridge gap between

documents.title: ASPLOS 2025: Paper Summaries and Insights / Hot Research Directions and Emerging Topics: Many ASPLOS 2025 papers converge on making massive-scale AI practical – from efficient LLM serving and training of MoE experts, to new systems for privacy-preserving ML (homomorphic encryption on GPUs, zero-knowledge proofs acceleration). Another clear thrust is memory disaggregation and CXL: multiple works assume an era of composable memory (CXL-enabled) and propose new OS, file, and transaction system designs to exploit it. Security remains vibrant – but the focus is shifting from classic OS/network security to hardware-centric and microarchitectural issues like RowHammer, speculative execution, and side-channel-resistant scheduling. Serverless and cloud-native optimizations are also prominent: there’s interest in bringing high-performance techniques (GPU sharing, low-latency networking, global scheduling) into serverless and containerized environments, indicating an industry push toward combining performance with the flexibility of cloud abstractions.

documents.title: ASPLOS 2025: Paper Summaries and Insights / Unsolved Problems and Gaps: Despite progress, many papers underscore enduring challenges. Memory wall and data movement issues loom large – whether in inter-GPU communication for training or in persistent memory overheads. Several works (e.g., AnyKey KV-SSD, Fusion object store) illustrate that storage is still a bottleneck and custom solutions are needed to bridge the gap between CPU and data speeds. Security mitigations vs. performance trade-offs remain a tightrope walk: new RowHammer defenses and speculation tests must be ultra-lightweight to be adopted. The complexity of distributed systems management appears in work like Coach (multi-resource oversubscription) and Embracing Imbalance – suggesting that as cloud systems scale, automated, fine-grained control is still an open problem (especially ensuring those systems remain robust and stable under all conditions). In architectures, a gap exists between theoretical accelerators (e.g., analog in-memory computing, CGRAs) and full software integration – papers like Be CIM or Be Memory and PartIR try to fill this, but it’s clear that making exotic hardware easy to program is still in progress.

documents.title: ASPLOS 2025: Paper Summaries and Insights / Surprising Challenges Surfaced: One surprise is how critical system-level orchestration has become in non-traditional areas: e.g., the side-channel work on scheduling reveals the scheduler can inadvertently aid attacks, a challenge that OS designers didn’t originally anticipate. Another striking theme is the challenge of observability in modern systems – not just logging, but doing so without perturbing the system (EXIST’s ultra-low overhead tracing, BTrace on devices). It appears that simply seeing what our complex systems are doing (especially microservices and edge devices) is hard, and researchers are treating observability as a first-class problem. Consistency and correctness in new contexts came up too: e.g., fuzzing persistent memory programs – something classical fuzzing never dealt with – showing that once we add persistence or heterogeneity, even basic development tools need rethinking.

documents.title: ASPLOS 2025: Paper Summaries and Insights / Advances and Prevailing Trends (Architecture, Systems, PL): Across architecture and hardware: there’s a strong trend of domain-specific solutions – whether it’s a special atomic for graphics or co-designed hardware/software for database transactions, we see less one-size-fits-all and more specialized tailoring. Heterogeneity is assumed: many papers take for granted systems with CPUs, GPUs, FPGAs, smart NICs, PIM, and try to orchestrate across them (the max-flow LLM scheduler, or memory offload via NIC). In systems, cloud software is being infused with techniques from HPC (and vice versa): e.g., cloud schedulers employing fine-grained time-sharing (Coach) and HPC runtimes borrowing cloud elasticity (PartIR enabling multi-parallelism strategies, akin to cloud scaling). In PL, a notable trend is raising the level of abstraction without losing control – Exo 2’s extensible scheduling language and the einsum optimization abstraction both attempt to give programmers high-level power and low-level efficiency, reflecting modern PL design goals (think PyTorch’s eager mode with graph optimization – ease of use but optimized execution).

documents.title: ASPLOS 2025: Paper Summaries and Insights / Shifts in Assumptions and Design Principles: A subtle but important shift is the assumption that “software will have to work around hardware limits, and hardware will expose more to software.” For example, many works assume hardware will provide new hooks (counters per DRAM row, or CXL’s huge address space) and that software must intelligently exploit them. The line between components is blurring: kernel and application boundaries (as seen with user-driven preemption timing attacks or user-level thread scheduling in Coach) are being reexamined, meaning systems are increasingly co-designed end-to-end. There’s also a clear recognition that tailoring to context (context-aware optimizations) is key: static, one-size-fits-all solutions are out of favor. Instead, systems now monitor themselves and adapt (be it shifting microservice loads, or adjusting GPU quantization on the fly). This reflects a design principle of adaptivity – the best system is one that can sense and respond to workload changes in real time, often guided by ML or telemetry.

documents.title: ASPLOS 2025: Paper Summaries and Insights / Implications for Future Research and Opportunities: The breadth of ASPLOS 2025’s contributions points to an exciting convergence of fields. Future research will likely explore automation and AI in systems – e.g., using learning to manage resources (some papers already hint at it with learned invariant detection or auto-tuners like DarwinGame). The continued arms race in microarchitectural security will spur more cross-layer ideas (like combining software scheduling and hardware fixes). With CXL and disaggregation maturing, we can expect more research on OS and database techniques for a world where memory and storage are network-attached (the community will need to address consistency, security, and performance in those). The interest in privacy-preserving computation (HE, ZK proofs) indicates a nascent but growing area: making these techniques fast enough for real-world deployment is an open challenge that intersects architecture, PL, and cryptography – future work may include specialized chips for ZK or better compilers for encrypted computing.

documents.bodyTerms: this post offers detailed examination papers accepted eurosys 2025, one the premier conferences computer systems research. i've analyzed over 40 spanning systems, cloud computing, networking, storage, and security identify emerging trends, technical breakthroughs, industry implications. for researchers practitioners alike, analysis provides roadmap where research heading—highlighting both solved problems remaining challenges. each paper summarized with its key contributions practical relevance, followed synthesis overarching themes future directions. hot directions 2025 collection underscores ai/ml dominant theme. many address challenges training serving large models from distributed optimizations (mist, mepipe, hybridflow) efficient inference (hcache, pensieve, cacheblend, mac, spinfer, deltazip). trend towards bespoke support llms designing scheduling, memory management, compression techniques specifically giant models. also see using within themselves (deep learned indexes like loft). cross pollination (using build vice versa) clear trajectory work. another direction resource disaggregation tiering multiple (deft, chrono, pet, adios) tackle fundamental shifts needed when storage disaggregated across network split into tiers. community preparing world cxl attached pooled resources ensuring performance doesn’t crumble even hardware becomes more distributed. meticulous hotness tracking (chrono) yield based page fault handling (adios) illustrate broad effort rethink services microsecond scale networks. networking data center communication remains vibrant, new congestion control

documents.bodyTerms: modern computing increasingly relies specialized accelerators notably gpus dpus and apus handle diverse workloads. graphics processing unit (gpu) massively parallel processor originally for graphics, now essential general purpose (hpc) ai. data (dpu) newer class programmable combining cpu cores with high performance network/storage engines. offload networking, security, storage tasks from the cpu, are considered "third pillar" alongside cpus gpus. meanwhile, accelerated units (apus) integrate gpu components one chip approach pioneered amd's fusion architecture enabling unified memory throughput hpc these run range workloads: excel math (hpc simulation, deep learning training/inference, analytics) rendering graphics; focus centric (network packet processing, encryption, offload, virtualization); target heterogeneous workloads needing tight coupling (e.g. sharing multimedia applications). profiling tracing tools crucial optimizing accelerators. such collect low level hardware telemetry counters utilization, throughput, occupancy, cache misses) can perform instruction event (capturing timelines kernel executions, copies, network flows, etc.). goal identify bottlenecks inefficiencies both code domain specific pipelines (like model training, function rendering). however, highly parallel, systems presents challenges overhead, volume, cross platform compatibility. this review categorizes current tracing/profiling type domain, compares open source commercial solutions, highlights major projects recent research. also discuss toolsets (including ebpf based approaches akin linux's bcc) adapted

documents.title: WASI and the WebAssembly Component Model: Current Status
documents.description: WASI and the WebAssembly Component Model: Current Status
documents.titleText: wasi and the webassembly component model: current status
documents.descriptionText: wasi and the webassembly component model: current status wasi and the webassembly component model: current status
documents.bodyTerms: webassembly (wasm) has evolved from browser based technology into promising runtime for server and embedded applications. key this evolution are the system interface (wasi) standardized set syscalls wasm outside emerging component model which enables modular, language agnostic composition modules. report analyzes current status wasi (including its major runtimes like wasmtime wasmer) model, focusing technical limitations that impede adoption. draw official proposals, issue trackers, academic studies, industry experiences highlight critical gaps, unresolved bugs, feature requests. case studies illustrate how these challenges block real world use cases, conclude with recommendations improving webassembly’s performance, security, integrability host environments. overview overview: modular gives modules controlled access operating features such files, clocks, networking. was created extend beyond browsers, where vanilla had direct due sandboxing (1) (1). early versions (wasi “snapshot preview1”) provided basic posix functions (e.g. file io, environment vars, random) but notably lacked capabilities networking threading. 2024 bytecode alliance released preview 2 (also called 0.2) iteration incorporates expands available apis (2) (2). 0.2 introduced “worlds” cohesive sets interfaces specific domains. example: cli command line apps (arguments, environment) (2), http outbound requests filesystem files directories sockets tcp/udp socket support others clocks random number generation broadened api surface

documents.bodyTerms: introduction and summary extended berkeley packet filter (ebpf) continues rapidly evolve, cementing its role cornerstone for operating system extensibility. 2024 into early 2025, the ebpf ecosystem saw significant advancements across linux kernel, tooling, security, networking, observability domains. this report provides comprehensive technical deep dive these developments, with high level here detailed sections below. key highlights include: generated from openai reasearch. let's see how performs ebpf. kernel enhancements: gained new features like bpf tokens safer unprivileged usage arenas shared memory, well exceptions improve program logic powered scheduler. performance verifier improvements have bolstered stability capabilities (1) (2). tooling improvements: development experience was enhanced updated libraries (libbpf), tracing tools (bcc, bpftrace), language bindings (e.g. rust), making easier write, debug, deploy programs. improved compile once run anywhere (co re) workflows debugging utilities were focus. security applications: increasingly used runtime monitoring detecting threats via hooks, enforcing policies module (lsm). tracee tetragon leverage provide visibility enforcement, offering dynamic policy control without patches (3: ebpf/bpf lsm accuknox). sandboxing techniques least privilege enhancements (like tokens) make use multi tenant environments. networking innovations: has further transformed period, improving processing flexibility. xdp (express data path) other based hooks enable handling millions packets

documents.bodyTerms: are excited introduce llvmbpf new project aimed empowering developers with high performance, multi architecture ebpf virtual machine (vm) that leverages the llvm framework for just time (jit) and ahead (aot) compilation. this component part bpftime but focuses solely core vm. operates standalone library compiler tool. optimized flexibility, minimal dependencies, making easy integrate into various environments without unnecessary overhead. why llvmbpf? although there several userspace runtimes available, built address specific needs existing solutions may not fully satisfy: 1. aot ability compile bytecode native elf object files allows deploy pre compiled programs, ensuring performance efficiency, especially resource constrained environments. additionally, can allow you experiment different optimization techniques based ir, providing more flexibility control over compilation process. 2. deployment llvmbpf, build programs binaries don’t require external dependencies. feature particularly useful deploying embedded systems, microcontrollers, other where installing additional software impractical. compared code development, ensures verified after integration verifier. 3. all support designed compatible across multiple architectures, versatile wide range hardware platforms. 4. maps relocation unlike many solutions, provides robust maps, data relocation, lddw helper functions, allowing creation complex powerful programs. 5. extensible approaches leveraging llvm’s capabilities, advanced optimizations such inlining well using original enhanced performance.

documents.bodyTerms: this blog post mainly references alexei starovoitov's presentation "modernize bpf for the next 10 years" bpfconf 2024. imagine having swiss army knife your computer's core operations—something that lets you peek inside how data moves, tweak processes fly, and monitor everything real time. that’s exactly what ebpf (extended berkeley packet filter) offers. over past decade, has transformed from simple filtering tool into powerhouse networking, observability, security. so, what’s ebpf? let’s dive its journey, explore where it’s headed ten years, discuss challenges opportunities lie ahead. exploration will help understand shaping future modern systems. decade review: ebpf's journey far did programmable networking begin with back 2014, world was facing some serious limitations. traditional stacks were rigid, making tough implement custom processing logic tailored specific needs. enter ebpf—a game changer allowed developers write small programs running directly kernel whenever network arrived. innovation meant more control handling, leading better performance flexibility without hassle cumbersome drivers. ebpf, could create solutions fit their exact requirements, paving way efficient innovative applications. shift marked beginning customization hand hand. made xdp high speed networking? gained traction significant hurdle emerged: buff structure linux introduced too much overhead, difficult achieve speeds like gbps. while

documents.title: Progress of eunomia-bpf in March
documents.description: The eunomia-bpf project is an open-source project aimed at providing a set of tools for writing and running eBPF programs more conveniently in the Linux kernel. In the past month, the project has made some new progress.
documents.titleText: progress of eunomia-bpf in march
documents.descriptionText: the eunomia-bpf project is an open-source project aimed at providing a set of tools for writing and running ebpf programs more conveniently in the linux kernel. in the past month, the project has made some new progress. the eunomia-bpf project is an open-source project aimed at providing a set of tools for writing and running ebpf programs more conveniently in the linux kernel. in the past month, the project has made some new progress.

documents.title: 如何在 Linux 显微镜（LMP）项目中开启 eBPF 之旅？
documents.description: eBPF 为 Linux 内核提供了可扩展性，使开发人员能够对 Linux 内核进行编程，以便根据他们的业务需求快速构建智能的或丰富的功能。
documents.href: /blog/2022/10/11/如何在-linux-显微镜-lmp-项目中开启-ebpf-之旅/
documents.titleText: 如何在 linux 显微镜（lmp）项目中开启 ebpf 之旅？
documents.descriptionText: ebpf 为 linux 内核提供了可扩展性，使开发人员能够对 linux 内核进行编程，以便根据他们的业务需求快速构建智能的或丰富的功能。 ebpf 为 linux 内核提供了可扩展性，使开发人员能够对 linux 内核进行编程，以便根据他们的业务需求快速构建智能的或丰富的功能。
documents.bodyTerms: ebpf linux 内核提供了可扩展性，使开发人员能够对 内核进行编程，以便根据他们的业务需求快速构建智能的或丰富的功能。 我们的 lmp(linux microscope) 是为了充分挖掘 的可能性而建立的，项目以构建 学习社区、成为 工具集散地、孵化 想法和项目为目标，正在大力建设中。之前我们在 lmp 其中的 supermarket 中包含了大量由个人开发者编写的 工具，覆盖了网络、性能分析、安全等多种功能，我们正在尝试把其中的一些程序迁移到 hub，一些规范化的 程序库，可以随时下载运行，或嵌入大型应用程序中作为插件使用。 我们尝试在 hub 中，基于 eunomia bpf 开发框架创建符合 oci 标准的 wasm 程序，并利用 oras 简化扩展 分发、加载、运行能力。 快速使用 如果您想快速开始 ebpf，可以使用我们开发的轻量级框架之上的命令行程序 cli。当使用脚本安装好我们的框架之后，您只需要一条命令，无需任何编译，即可体会到 的强大之处： run sigsnoop download with curl: https://linuxkerneltravel.github.io/lmp/sigsnoop/package.json running and waiting for the events from perf event... time pid tpid sig ret comm 0041 109955 112863 28 0 gnome terminal 112862 ... 如果您使用过 bcc 开发工具，您一定会惊喜于 的便捷性。lmp 中包含了各种各样的 程序，这种便捷的运行，离不开我们基于的底层框架 bpf，它完全实现了“一次编译，处处运行”的 跨平台目标。在 框架下，lmp 开发的 应用不仅可以适配任意架构和不同内核版本，而且还具有轻量级、良好的隔离性等优点，可以作为插件到嵌入大型应用之中。 bpf：结合 的轻量级开发框架 作为一个 程序的轻量级开发加载框架，eunomia 运行时和 btf 技术，包含了一个用户态动态加载框架/运行时库，以及一个简单的编译 字节码的工具链容器。 是为了一个可移植的目标而设计的，可作为 c/c+/rust 等高级语言的编译目标，使客户端和服务器应用程序能够在 web 上部署。目前已经发展成为一个轻量级、高性能、跨平台和多语种的软件沙盒环境，被运用于云原生软件组件。 用户态的所有控制和数据处理逻辑全部移到 虚拟机中，通过 module 打包和分发 字节码，同时在 虚拟机内部控制整个 程序的加载和执行，将二者的优势结合了起来。 模块中编写 代码和通常熟悉的使用 libbpf 框架或 coolbpf 程序的方式是基本一样的，wasm 的复杂性会被隐藏在 的编译工具链和运行时库中，开发者可以专注于 程序的开发和调试，不需要了解 的背景知识，也不需要担心 的编译环境配置。 大致来说，eunomia 运行时和用户态的 中间多加了一层抽象层，使得一次编译、到处运行的 代码可以从 json 对象中动态加载。json 对象会在编译时被包含在 模块中，因此在运行时，我们可以通过解析 对象来获取 程序的信息，然后动态加载 程序。通过 程序的加载和执行，eunomia 就可以将二者的优势结合起来，让任意 程序能有如下特性： 可移植：让 工具和应用不需要进行重新编译即可以跨平台分发，省去了复杂的交叉编译流程； 隔离性：让 程序的加载和执行、以及用户态的数据处理流程更加安全可靠。 包管理：完成 程序或工具的分发、管理、加载等工作。 敏捷性：使每个人都可以使用官方和未经修改的应用程序来加载自定义扩展，任何 程序的错误修复和/或更新都可以在运行时推送和/或测试，而不需要更新和/或重新部署一个新的二进制。 轻量级：与 容器应用相比，wasm 微服务冷启动的时间是 1%，可以实现 service，让 程序的加载和执行变得更加轻量级、快速、简便易行。 我们已经测试了在 x86、arm 等不同架构不同内核版本的 系统上，eunomia 框架都可以使用同一个预编译 程序二进制，从云端一行命令获取到本地之后运行。之后 还会添加 risc 等更多架构的支持。 cli 构建一个 如果您是一个 工具的使用者，您可以无需任何编译流程，也不需要了解任何 的相关知识，使用 就可以直接运行 仓库的小程序，其中会调用lmp pull 命令从云端从库中下载对应的小程序。 程序的开发者，让我们开始创建、编译并运行一个简单的程序。在这里，我们使用基简单命令行工具 cli，概述如何从四个步骤开始构建。 1. 准备你的环境 本身是一种 内核技术，因此任何实际的 程序都必须在 内核中运行。我建议您从内核 5.4 或更新的版本开始。从 ssh 终端，检查内核版本，并确认您已经启用了 config debug info btf： uname cat /boot/config $(uname grep 你会看到类似这样的输出： 5.15.0 48 generic btf=y modules=y 安装命令行工具 cli： curl https://github.com/gorilamond/lmp cli/releases/download/lmp/install.sh 2. 项目的内核部分 使用lmp init创建一个项目模板，来初始化你的内核程序，快速地投入到代码的编写中： init

documents.descriptionText: ebpf (extended berkeley packet filter) is a high-performance kernel virtual machine that runs in the kernel space and is used to collect system and network information. with the continuous development of computer technol ebpf (extended berkeley packet filter) is a high-performance kernel virtual machine that runs in the kernel space and is used to collect system and network information. with the continuous development of computer technol
documents.bodyTerms: authors: tong, zheng yusheng ebpf (extended berkeley packet filter) high performance kernel virtual machine that runs the space and used collect system network information. with continuous development computer technology, has become increasingly powerful build various efficient online diagnostic tracing systems, well secure networks service meshes. webassembly (wasm) was initially developed for browser security sandbox purposes. now, evolved into performance, cross platform, multi language software environment cloud native components. lightweight nature wasm containers makes them suitable running next generation serverless platform runtime execution resource constrained scenarios such edge computing. help bpf compilation toolchain runtime, can use write programs modules, while using c/c++ rust programs. webassembly, not only enable applications benefit from access interfaces ebpf, but also allow leverage sandboxing, flexibility, nature, dynamic loading wasm. additionally, conveniently quickly distribute manage oci images. combining these two technologies will provide completely new experience ecosystems! writing, dynamically loading, distributing open source project: https://github.com/eunomia bpf/wasm bpf. defines abstraction related provides corresponding toolchain, library, general instance. similar libbpf bootstrap, automatically generating skeleton header files data structure definitions unordered communication between ebpf. this you easily your own any platform. more details, please refer our previous blog post: bpf: bridging programmability.

documents.title: 在 WebAssembly 中使用 Rust 编写 eBPF 程序并发布 OCI 镜像
documents.description: 在 WebAssembly 中使用 Rust 编写 eBPF 程序并发布 OCI 镜像
documents.href: /blog/2022/02/11/在-webassembly-中使用-rust-编写-ebpf-程序并发布-oci-镜像/
documents.titleText: 在 webassembly 中使用 rust 编写 ebpf 程序并发布 oci 镜像
documents.descriptionText: 在 webassembly 中使用 rust 编写 ebpf 程序并发布 oci 镜像 在 webassembly 中使用 rust 编写 ebpf 程序并发布 oci 镜像
documents.bodyTerms: 作者：于桐，郑昱笙 ebpf（extended berkeley packet filter）是一种高性能的内核虚拟机，可以运行在内核空间中，以收集系统和网络信息。随着计算机技术的不断发展，ebpf 的功能日益强大，并且已经成为各种效率高效的在线诊断和跟踪系统，以及构建安全的网络、服务网格的重要组成部分。 webassembly（wasm）最初是以浏览器安全沙盒为目的开发的，发展到目前为止，webassembly 已经成为一个用于云原生软件组件的高性能、跨平台和多语言软件沙箱环境，wasm 轻量级容器也非常适合作为下一代无服务器平台运行时，或在边缘计算等资源受限的场景高效执行。 现在，借助 wasm bpf 编译工具链和运行时，我们可以使用 ebpf 程序编写为跨平台的模块，使用 c/c++ rust 编写程序。通过在 webassembly 中使用 程序，我们不仅让 应用获得 的高性能、对系统接口的访问能力，还可以让 程序享受到 的沙箱、灵活性、跨平台性、和动态加载的能力，并且使用 oci 镜像来方便、快捷地分发和管理 程序。例如，可以类似 docker 一样，从云端一行命令获取 轻量级容器镜像，并运行任意 程序。通过结合这两种技术，我们将会给 生态来一个全新的开发体验！ 工具链在 中编写、动态加载、分发运行 在前两篇短文中，我们已经介绍了 的设计思路，以及如何使用 中编写 程序: bpf: 内核可编程的桥梁: libbpf wasm，我们可以使用多种语言构建 应用，并以统一、轻量级的方式管理和发布。以我们构建的示例应用 bootstrap.wasm 为例，使用 构建的镜像大小最小仅为 90k，很容易通过网络分发，并可以在不到 100ms 的时间内在另一台机器上动态部署、加载和运行，并且保留轻量级容器的隔离特性。运行时不需要内核特定版本头文件、llvm、clang 等依赖，也不需要做任何消耗资源的重量级的编译工作。对于 而言，编译产物会稍大一点，大约在 2m 左右。 本文将以 语言为例，讨论： 程序并编译为 镜像发布、部署、管理 程序，获得类似 的体验 我们在仓库中提供了几个示例程序，分别对应于可观测、网络、安全等多种场景。 的大致流程 一般说来，在非 沙箱的用户态空间，使用 bootstrap 脚手架，可以快速、轻松地使用 c/c++构建 应用程序。编译、构建和运行 程序（无论是采用什么语言），通常包含以下几个步骤： 编写内核态 程序的代码，一般使用 clang 编译器或者相关工具链编译 程序（要实现跨内核版本移植的话，需要包含 btf 信息）。 在用户态的开发程序中，编写对应的加载、控制、挂载、数据处理逻辑； 在实际运行的阶段，从用户态将 程序加载进入内核，并实际执行。 可能是 生态系统中支持最好的语言。rust 不仅支持几个 编译目标，而且 wasmtime、spin、wagi 和其他许多 工具都是用 编写的。因此，我们也提供了 的开发示例： wasi 生态系统非常棒 编写的，这意味着有大量的代码可以复用。 spin 通常在对其他语言的支持之前就有rust的功能支持 wasmtime rust编写的，通常在其他运行时之前就有最先进的功能。 可以在 中使用许多现成的 cargo 的灵活构建系统，一些 crates 甚至有特殊的功能标志来启用wasm的功能（例如chrono）。 的内存管理技术，与同类语言相比，rust 的二进制大小很小。 我们同样提供了一个 sdk，可以使用 的用户态程序并编译为 wasm。借助 aya 提供的相关工具链支持，内核态的 程序也可以用 进行编写，不过在这里，我们还是复用之前使用 语言编写的内核态程序。 首先，我们需要使用 提供的 工具链，创建一个新的项目： rustup target add wasm32 new helloworld 之后，可以使用 makefile make 完成整个编译流程，并生成 bootstrap.bpf.o 字节码文件。 wit bindgen 生成类型信息，用于内核态和 模块之间通信 documents.title: 在 WebAssembly 中使用 Rust 编写 eBPF 程序并发布 OCI 镜像
documents.description: eBPF（extended Berkeley Packet Filter）是一种高性能的内核虚拟机，可以运行在内核空间中，以收集系统和网络信息。随着计算机技术的不断发展，eBPF 的功能日益强大，并且已经成为各种效率高效的在线诊断和跟踪系统，以及构建安全的网络、服务网格的重要组成部分。
documents.titleText: 在 webassembly 中使用 rust 编写 ebpf 程序并发布 oci 镜像
documents.descriptionText: ebpf（extended berkeley packet filter）是一种高性能的内核虚拟机，可以运行在内核空间中，以收集系统和网络信息。随着计算机技术的不断发展，ebpf 的功能日益强大，并且已经成为各种效率高效的在线诊断和跟踪系统，以及构建安全的网络、服务网格的重要组成部分。 ebpf（extended berkeley packet filter）是一种高性能的内核虚拟机，可以运行在内核空间中，以收集系统和网络信息。随着计算机技术的不断发展，ebpf 的功能日益强大，并且已经成为各种效率高效的在线诊断和跟踪系统，以及构建安全的网络、服务网格的重要组成部分。
documents.bodyTerms: 作者：于桐，郑昱笙 ebpf（extended berkeley packet filter）是一种高性能的内核虚拟机，可以运行在内核空间中，以收集系统和网络信息。随着计算机技术的不断发展，ebpf 的功能日益强大，并且已经成为各种效率高效的在线诊断和跟踪系统，以及构建安全的网络、服务网格的重要组成部分。 webassembly（wasm）最初是以浏览器安全沙盒为目的开发的，发展到目前为止，webassembly 已经成为一个用于云原生软件组件的高性能、跨平台和多语言软件沙箱环境，wasm 轻量级容器也非常适合作为下一代无服务器平台运行时，或在边缘计算等资源受限的场景高效执行。 现在，借助 wasm bpf 编译工具链和运行时，我们可以使用 ebpf 程序编写为跨平台的模块，使用 c/c++ rust 编写程序。通过在 webassembly 中使用 程序，我们不仅让 应用获得 的高性能、对系统接口的访问能力，还可以让 程序享受到 的沙箱、灵活性、跨平台性、和动态加载的能力，并且使用 oci 镜像来方便、快捷地分发和管理 程序。例如，可以类似 docker 一样，从云端一行命令获取 轻量级容器镜像，并运行任意 程序。通过结合这两种技术，我们将会给 生态来一个全新的开发体验！ 工具链在 中编写、动态加载、分发运行 在前两篇短文中，我们已经介绍了 的设计思路，以及如何使用 中编写 程序: bpf: 内核可编程的桥梁: libbpf wasm，我们可以使用多种语言构建 应用，并以统一、轻量级的方式管理和发布。以我们构建的示例应用 bootstrap.wasm 为例，使用 构建的镜像大小最小仅为 90k，很容易通过网络分发，并可以在不到 100ms 的时间内在另一台机器上动态部署、加载和运行，并且保留轻量级容器的隔离特性。运行时不需要内核特定版本头文件、llvm、clang 等依赖，也不需要做任何消耗资源的重量级的编译工作。对于 而言，编译产物会稍大一点，大约在 2m 左右。 本文将以 语言为例，讨论： 程序并编译为 镜像发布、部署、管理 程序，获得类似 的体验 我们在仓库中提供了几个示例程序，分别对应于可观测、网络、安全等多种场景。 的大致流程 一般说来，在非 沙箱的用户态空间，使用 bootstrap 脚手架，可以快速、轻松地使用 c/c++构建 应用程序。编译、构建和运行 程序（无论是采用什么语言），通常包含以下几个步骤： 编写内核态 程序的代码，一般使用 clang 编译器或者相关工具链编译 程序（要实现跨内核版本移植的话，需要包含 btf 信息）。 在用户态的开发程序中，编写对应的加载、控制、挂载、数据处理逻辑； 在实际运行的阶段，从用户态将 程序加载进入内核，并实际执行。 可能是 生态系统中支持最好的语言。rust 不仅支持几个 编译目标，而且 wasmtime、spin、wagi 和其他许多 工具都是用 编写的。因此，我们也提供了 的开发示例： wasi 生态系统非常棒 编写的，这意味着有大量的代码可以复用。 spin 通常在对其他语言的支持之前就有rust的功能支持 wasmtime rust编写的，通常在其他运行时之前就有最先进的功能。 可以在 中使用许多现成的 cargo 的灵活构建系统，一些 crates 甚至有特殊的功能标志来启用wasm的功能（例如chrono）。 的内存管理技术，与同类语言相比，rust 的二进制大小很小。 我们同样提供了一个 sdk，可以使用 的用户态程序并编译为 wasm。借助 aya 提供的相关工具链支持，内核态的 程序也可以用 进行编写，不过在这里，我们还是复用之前使用 语言编写的内核态程序。 首先，我们需要使用 提供的 工具链，创建一个新的项目： rustup target add wasm32 new helloworld 之后，可以使用 makefile make 完成整个编译流程，并生成 bootstrap.bpf.o 字节码文件。 wit bindgen 生成类型信息，用于内核态和 模块之间通信 项目是一套着眼于 webassembly，并使用组件模型的语言的绑定生成器。绑定是用 .wit 文件描述的，文件中描述了 模块导入、导出的函数和接口。我们可以 它来生成多种语言的类型定义，以便在内核态的 和用户态的 模块之间传递数据。 我们首先需要在 cargo.toml 配置文件中加入 binding guest 依赖： path "wasm binding" 这个包提供了 由运行时提供给 模块，用于加载和控制 程序的函数的绑定。 仓库中有提供。 [dependencies] git "https://github.com/bytecodealliance/wit bindgen", version "0.3.0" [patch.crates io] component {git "https://github.com/bytecodealliance/wasm tools", "0.5.0", rev "9640d187a73a516c42b532cf2a10ba5403df5946"} parser 这个包支持用 文件为 客户程序生成绑定。使用这个包的情况下，我们不需要再手动运行 bindgen。 接下来，我们使用 btf2wit 工具，从 信息生成 文件。可以使用 install 安装我们提供的 工具，并编译生成 信息： event def.c event.def.o def.wit ../wit/ 是包含了我们需要的结构体信息的的 程序文件。只有在导出符号中用到的结构体才会被记录在 结构体生成的 信息，大致如下： default

documents.description: We are excited to introduce llvmbpf, a new project aimed at empowering developers with a high-performance, multi-architecture eBPF virtual machine (VM) that leverages the LLVM framework for Just-In-Time (JIT) and Ahead-O
documents.titleText: building high-performance userspace ebpf vms with llvm
documents.descriptionText: we are excited to introduce llvmbpf, a new project aimed at empowering developers with a high-performance, multi-architecture ebpf virtual machine (vm) that leverages the llvm framework for just-in-time (jit) and ahead-o we are excited to introduce llvmbpf, a new project aimed at empowering developers with a high-performance, multi-architecture ebpf virtual machine (vm) that leverages the llvm framework for just-in-time (jit) and ahead-o
documents.bodyTerms: are excited introduce llvmbpf new project aimed empowering developers with high performance, multi architecture ebpf virtual machine (vm) that leverages the llvm framework for just time (jit) and ahead (aot) compilation. this component part bpftime but focuses solely core vm. operates standalone library compiler tool. optimized flexibility, minimal dependencies, making easy integrate into various environments without unnecessary overhead. why llvmbpf? although there several userspace runtimes available, built address specific needs existing solutions may not fully satisfy: 1. aot ability compile bytecode native elf object files allows deploy pre compiled programs, ensuring performance efficiency, especially resource constrained environments. additionally, can allow you experiment different optimization techniques based ir, providing more flexibility control over compilation process. 2. deployment llvmbpf, build programs binaries don’t require external dependencies. feature particularly useful deploying embedded systems, microcontrollers, other where installing additional software impractical. compared code development, ensures verified after integration verifier. 3. all support designed compatible across multiple architectures, versatile wide range hardware platforms. 4. maps relocation unlike many solutions, provides robust maps, data relocation, lddw helper functions, allowing creation complex powerful programs. 5. extensible approaches leveraging llvm’s capabilities, advanced optimizations such inlining well using original enhanced performance.

documents.title: 如何在 Linux 显微镜（LMP）项目中开启 eBPF 之旅？
documents.description: eBPF 为 Linux 内核提供了可扩展性，使开发人员能够对 Linux 内核进行编程，以便根据他们的业务需求快速构建智能的或丰富的功能。
documents.titleText: 如何在 linux 显微镜（lmp）项目中开启 ebpf 之旅？
documents.descriptionText: ebpf 为 linux 内核提供了可扩展性，使开发人员能够对 linux 内核进行编程，以便根据他们的业务需求快速构建智能的或丰富的功能。 ebpf 为 linux 内核提供了可扩展性，使开发人员能够对 linux 内核进行编程，以便根据他们的业务需求快速构建智能的或丰富的功能。
documents.bodyTerms: ebpf linux 内核提供了可扩展性，使开发人员能够对 内核进行编程，以便根据他们的业务需求快速构建智能的或丰富的功能。 我们的 lmp(linux microscope) 是为了充分挖掘 的可能性而建立的，项目以构建 学习社区、成为 工具集散地、孵化 想法和项目为目标，正在大力建设中。之前我们在 lmp 其中的 supermarket 中包含了大量由个人开发者编写的 工具，覆盖了网络、性能分析、安全等多种功能，我们正在尝试把其中的一些程序迁移到 hub，一些规范化的 程序库，可以随时下载运行，或嵌入大型应用程序中作为插件使用。 我们尝试在 hub 中，基于 eunomia bpf 开发框架创建符合 oci 标准的 wasm 程序，并利用 oras 简化扩展 分发、加载、运行能力。 快速使用 如果您想快速开始 ebpf，可以使用我们开发的轻量级框架之上的命令行程序 cli。当使用脚本安装好我们的框架之后，您只需要一条命令，无需任何编译，即可体会到 的强大之处： run sigsnoop download with curl: https://linuxkerneltravel.github.io/lmp/sigsnoop/package.json running and waiting for the events from perf event... time pid tpid sig ret comm 0041 109955 112863 28 0 gnome terminal 112862 ... 如果您使用过 bcc 开发工具，您一定会惊喜于 的便捷性。lmp 中包含了各种各样的 程序，这种便捷的运行，离不开我们基于的底层框架 bpf，它完全实现了“一次编译，处处运行”的 跨平台目标。在 框架下，lmp 开发的 应用不仅可以适配任意架构和不同内核版本，而且还具有轻量级、良好的隔离性等优点，可以作为插件到嵌入大型应用之中。 bpf：结合 的轻量级开发框架 作为一个 程序的轻量级开发加载框架，eunomia 运行时和 btf 技术，包含了一个用户态动态加载框架/运行时库，以及一个简单的编译 字节码的工具链容器。 是为了一个可移植的目标而设计的，可作为 c/c+/rust 等高级语言的编译目标，使客户端和服务器应用程序能够在 web 上部署。目前已经发展成为一个轻量级、高性能、跨平台和多语种的软件沙盒环境，被运用于云原生软件组件。 用户态的所有控制和数据处理逻辑全部移到 虚拟机中，通过 module 打包和分发 字节码，同时在 虚拟机内部控制整个 程序的加载和执行，将二者的优势结合了起来。 模块中编写 代码和通常熟悉的使用 libbpf 框架或 coolbpf 程序的方式是基本一样的，wasm 的复杂性会被隐藏在 的编译工具链和运行时库中，开发者可以专注于 程序的开发和调试，不需要了解 的背景知识，也不需要担心 的编译环境配置。 大致来说，eunomia 运行时和用户态的 中间多加了一层抽象层，使得一次编译、到处运行的 代码可以从 json 对象中动态加载。json 对象会在编译时被包含在 模块中，因此在运行时，我们可以通过解析 对象来获取 程序的信息，然后动态加载 程序。通过 程序的加载和执行，eunomia 就可以将二者的优势结合起来，让任意 程序能有如下特性： 可移植：让 工具和应用不需要进行重新编译即可以跨平台分发，省去了复杂的交叉编译流程； 隔离性：让 程序的加载和执行、以及用户态的数据处理流程更加安全可靠。 包管理：完成 程序或工具的分发、管理、加载等工作。 敏捷性：使每个人都可以使用官方和未经修改的应用程序来加载自定义扩展，任何 程序的错误修复和/或更新都可以在运行时推送和/或测试，而不需要更新和/或重新部署一个新的二进制。 轻量级：与 容器应用相比，wasm 微服务冷启动的时间是 1%，可以实现 service，让 程序的加载和执行变得更加轻量级、快速、简便易行。 我们已经测试了在 x86、arm 等不同架构不同内核版本的 系统上，eunomia 框架都可以使用同一个预编译 程序二进制，从云端一行命令获取到本地之后运行。之后 还会添加 risc 等更多架构的支持。 cli 构建一个 如果您是一个 工具的使用者，您可以无需任何编译流程，也不需要了解任何 的相关知识，使用 就可以直接运行 仓库的小程序，其中会调用lmp pull 命令从云端从库中下载对应的小程序。 程序的开发者，让我们开始创建、编译并运行一个简单的程序。在这里，我们使用基简单命令行工具 cli，概述如何从四个步骤开始构建。 1. 准备你的环境 本身是一种 内核技术，因此任何实际的 程序都必须在 内核中运行。我建议您从内核 5.4 或更新的版本开始。从 ssh 终端，检查内核版本，并确认您已经启用了 config debug info btf： uname cat /boot/config $(uname grep 你会看到类似这样的输出： 5.15.0 48 generic btf=y modules=y 安装命令行工具 cli： curl https://github.com/gorilamond/lmp cli/releases/download/lmp/install.sh 2. 项目的内核部分 使用lmp init创建一个项目模板，来初始化你的内核程序，快速地投入到代码的编写中： init
